title: Efficient and Scalable Agentic AI with Heterogeneous Hardware
preview: Examines how heterogeneous hardware configurations (GPU+CPU+memory accelerator) improve efficiency for the mixed workload profile of agentic AI pipelines.
summary: Analyses the compute profile of agentic AI: rapid tool dispatch (CPU-bound), retrieval (memory-bound), and LLM inference (GPU-bound). Proposes heterogeneous scheduling that assigns each step to the appropriate processor type, reducing E2E latency and GPU idle time compared to GPU-only deployments.
datacenter: Relevant to rack-level architecture decisions for agentic AI deployment. Points toward tiered compute designs with fast CPU cores, high-bandwidth memory, and GPU accelerators in the same chassis — impacting server OEM selection and rack topology.
metrics: Key result signal: end-to-end latency and GPU utilisation improvement for agentic pipelines on heterogeneous versus homogeneous configurations.
